column1: /Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:1:In Tetris Attack, it seems like a panel cannot match in the same frame
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:3:attack by performing the matches check before the other state transitions.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:12:the new swap can only be created in some frames of the old swap.  In
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:13:panel-attack, swaps cannot occur on consecutive frames, but I think
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:17:In Tetris Attack, you get nothing for chains past 13.  I will probably
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:24:panels the player clears, while panel-attack's is not.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:28:In Tetris Attack, a stack of garbage that should all begin falling at the same
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:29:time will occasionally separate.  panel-attack will not implement this bug.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:32:by a match will finish after the match and rise lock end.  panel-attack
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/differences.txt:35:In Panel de Pon (but not in Tetris Attack), a 32 combo gives too many points.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:3:When a player makes a combo or chain, garbage is sent to the opponent.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:7:The amount of garbage is as follows:
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:22:K metal blocks in combo: (K-2)M in addition to normal combo garbage
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:24:For the purposes of this document, assume P1 is dropping garbage on P2.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:26:The garbage is not dropped instantly once it is awarded.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:28:stack N frames after it is awarded.     #TODO: find N.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:30:First, the garbage will wait for P1's chain to end.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:32:Then all the garbage that was created during P1's chain will wait until
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:33:the most recent combo or chain (in this set of garbage)
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:34:was at least M frames ago.              #TODO: find M.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:36:Then, all the garbage will wait until P2's chain to end.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:38:At this time, after Q frames, the       #TODO: find Q.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:43:    chain is of any respectable length.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:44:- Multiple chains worth of garbage may accumulate in the third
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:45:    stage if P1 makes short chains or unchained combos
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:46:    while P2 is making a long chain.
/Users/tatsheen/claw-repos/oss-index/puzzle-bench/panel-attack/GARBAGE.txt:48:To find precise garbage timings I will probably have to use a debugger.
